Alexander the Great Not So Great
TimelineThe Philip Factorvs NapoleonSources

Interactive Timeline

Click on events to learn more. Events marked in gold show Philip II's contributions. Events in bronze show Alexander's actions.

382 BC

Philip II Born

Philip II of Macedon is born, destined to transform Macedonia

356 BC

Alexander Born

Alexander is born into an already powerful kingdom

338 BC

Battle of Chaeronea

Philip's decisive victory unifies Greece

336 BC

Philip Assassinated

Alexander inherits a fully prepared war machine

334 BC

Granicus River

First major battle in Persia using Philip's army

333 BC

Battle of Issus

Defeat of Darius III

331 BC

Battle of Gaugamela

Conquest of the Persian Empire

323 BC

Alexander Dies

Empire fragments within years

Key Insight

Notice the pattern: every major Macedonian victory from 356-323 BC was achieved using the army, tactics, and resources that Philip II built over decades. Alexander inherited a war machine fine-tuned by his father. Without Philip's innovations, there would be no "Alexander the Great."
